rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT DAVID WONG, MICHAEL ZEHNER, BRIAN SAVAGE, SHEDRICK MCDANIELS, SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S FOUNDATION MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. As an initial matter, Defendants set the hearing over three months after the moving papers were filed, in violation of CCP sec. 425.16(f). Defendants have provided no explanation of why a hearing date within 30 days of filing was not obtained. The motion is also denied on the merits. CCP sec. 425.16(b)(1) explicitly provides that an Anti-SLAPP motion is directed to a cause of action. Defendants seek to attack factual allegations with their Anti-SLAPP motion, but provide no authority that an Anti-SLAPP motion can be used in such a way. In the First Amended Complaint, Plaintiffs have struck the causes of action to which the original Anti-SLAPP motion was granted. As to the remaining causes of action for violation of the Corporation Code and concealment (causes of action nos. 6 and 8), Defendants engage in no meaningful analysis as to how the causes of action arise from protected activity. As such, the first prong of CCP sec. 425.16 is not met. The Court finds that this Anti-SLAPP motion is frivolous and awards the fees and costs incurred by Plaintiffs in opposing this motion, pursuant to CCP sec. 425.16(c)(1). Plaintiffs may file a motion to recover their reasonable attorney's fees and costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT DAVID WONG, MICHAEL ZEHNER, BRIAN SAVAGE, SHEDRICK MCDANIELS, SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S FOUNDATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Although the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") was filed before the remittitur from the Court of Appeals was entered, the Court currently has jurisdiction over the FAC, and it would serve little practical purpose to require Plaintiffs to re-file the complaint. A demurrer is not the proper procedural mechanism to remove factual allegations from a complaint, such as the allegations pertaining to the individual defendants. Additionally, Defendants cite no authority that a demurrer may be used to remove class action allegations from a complaint. The demurrer is overruled as to the 6th and 8th causes of actions. The FAC adequately pleads facts to state a cause of action for violation of Corp. Code secs. 6333 and 6334, as previously held in the Court's September 12, 2013 Order on Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings. The FAC adequately pleads facts to state a cause of action for concealment. rulings.Case Title: 2900 22ND STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. SANTANA REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Cross-Complaint Jointly Filed By Santana Real Estate Development Esquivel Santana
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 12, INTERVENOR NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Cross-Complaint Jointly Filed By Santana Real Estate Development Esquivel Santana. The motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the developer (Santana Real Estate Development, LLC) and guarantor (Esquivel Santana) agreed to hold harmless the general contractor (Matrix Construction). (See Par. 9 of Addendum 1 and Guaranty). Matrix Construction's insurer (moving party North American Specialty Insurance Company) is entitled to rely on that covenant and is accordingly entitled to summary judgment. See Reliance Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 387-388. The court overrules the opposing parties' evidentiary objections. rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Danny Buist And Pacific Gas And Electric Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 16, Defendants Danny Buist And Pacific Gas And Electric Company's Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication. Duplicate entry. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT DANNY BUIST, PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication. The court grants summary judgment. Defendant has maintained its summary adjudication burden on the wrongful termination, discrimination, and retaliation causes of action (causes of action 2-7). Paragraphs 48-51 of the Buist declaration and paragraph 18 of the Wix declaration show that Buist, Wix, and Wix's manager made the decision to terminate plaintiff based on performance issues, including customer complaints. Paragraph 15 of the Wix declaration shows that plaintiff's performance issues were far worse than similarly situated employees. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Fact ("UMF") 124). The burden of production has thus shifted to plaintiff to show pretext. Plaintiff does not sufficiently counter defendant's evidence and demonstrate pretext. "[T]o avoid summary judgment, an employee claiming discrimination must offer substantial evidence that the employer's stated non-discriminatory reason for the adverse action was untrue or pretextual, or evidence the employer acted with a discriminatory animus, or a combination of the two, such that a reasonable trier of fact could conclude the employer engaged in intentional discrimination." Hersant v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-1005. In opposition, plaintiff fails to meet her burden. At Defendant's UMF 119, plaintiff admits that the only employee with a discriminatory animus against her was Mr. Buist. Plaintiff cites Mr. Buist's allegedly sexist and ageist comments. As in Serri v. Santa Clara University (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 830, 868, the vague comments do not create a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff's evidence does not demonstrate a discriminatory animus or motive. At best, even when examined in the aggregate, this evidence raised only a weak suspicion that discrimination was a basis for plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff also cannot demonstrate the retaliation cause of action. The temporal proximity between plaintiff's protected conduct and the adverse employment action alone is insufficient to avoid summary judgment. See Arteaga v. Brink's Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 327, 334. The court grants summary adjudication on the harassment cause of action. The cited statements and conduct are insufficiently pervasive, derogatory, and offensive to amount to harassment based upon age or sex under the Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA). See Reno v. Baird, (1998) 18 Cal.4th 646-647. rulings.Case Title: BRUCE LEE CEARLOCK et al VS. ALYCE D. CRAFT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, With Request For Sanctions; Separate Statement Of Items In Dispute; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Jennifer J. Capabianco; And [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT ALYCE CRAFT'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, With Request For Sanctions. Page 1 of 2: Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Alyce D. Craft's motion is granted. No opposition was filed and Plaintiffs failed to engage or respond to Defendant's attempts to meet and confer to resolve this discovery dispute. Defendant is entitled to complete and code-compliant responses to her discovery demands in order to prepare her defense. Plaintiffs shall provide a further, verified response to Defendant's Requests for Production of Documents with a code-compliant statement of compliance and shall, concurrent with serving further responses, produce all documents responsive to the Defendant's Requests for Production. Plaintiff Bruce Lee Cearlock shall provide further, verified responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatory numbered 9.1(b) and 9.1(c), 12.1 to correct errors in response, all subsections of 12.4, and 50.1(e). Plaintiff Nila Cearlock shall provide further, verified responses to Defendant's Form Interrogatory numbered 2.7(a), 8.2, 9.1(b) and 9.1(c), 12.1 to correct errors in response, all subsections of 12.4, and 50.1(e). Plaintiffs shall provide to Defendant all further, verified responses identified in this order, along with any additional production of documents, no later than January 9, 2015. Plaintiffs, and their counsel, are also sanctioned for misuse of the discovery process and for failing to meaningfully engage in the meet and confer process. They did not provide justification for their refusal to correct their responses to discovery and did not adequately respond to Defendant's attempts to resolve this dispute short of court intervention. Accordingly, Plaintiff Bruce Lee Cearlock, Plaintiff Nila Cearlock and their counsel are jointly and severally sanctioned in the amount of $1,380, payable to Defendant no later than January 20, 2015. Continued to Page 2 of 2 =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BAKER ET AL VS. NINA MACKENZIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demand For Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFFS DAVID BAKER, an architectural corporation, and DAVID BAKER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demand For Documents. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pursuant to the Court's order dated December 8, 2014, Plaintiff's motion is stayed pending the outcome of Plaintiff's Motion for Consolidation in case number PES-14-298241. Accordingly, Plaintiff's motion is taken off calendar and shall be re-noticed, if appropriate to do so, once the Court lifts the stay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
